subject: Mozi Minute: How To Open A Sales Call
body:
Words I like: You can't close what you do not open. Blow the intro, blow the sale.

Mozi Minute: How To Open A Sales Call

The intro is the first 30-60 seconds of every call. You can blow a 30-minute call in 30 seconds.

The Big Four (Required By Law):
- Your name
- Your company name
- That the call is recorded
- The reason you're calling

Then State The Agenda:
"You opted in for [lead magnet]. During this call we'll [thing 1, thing 2, thing 3], and at the end we'll see if it makes sense to move forward. If not - no sweat."

Full Intro Example:
"[Name]? Hey it's [Rep] from Acquisition.com calling about [Product] on a recorded line - how's it going?"
[Wait for response]
"Great. Excited for our call. We only have 20 minutes though. Cool if we jump in?"
[Wait for response]
"Let's do it. We'll spend a couple minutes diving through your metrics to make sure you're a fit. And if so, happy to walk you through [Product]. If things make sense we can get you all set up. Sound good?"

What The Intro Is NOT:
- Not small talk
- Not a pitch
- Not a place to lie, skip parts, or talk fast

What The Intro IS:
The minimum required questions, with the minimum required words, delivered in the proper tone.

Talk soon,
- Alex
